name: LENS @ JHU — Learning Engineering for Next-Generation Systems
affiliation_or_venue: Johns Hopkins University — School of Education / APL
description: Concentration within JHU MEd in Learning Design & Technology. Targets practitioners in complex organizations: defense, healthcare, large-scale education. Grounded in human systems integration and learning engineering expertise. Capstone produces evidence dashboard, reproducible report, and governance/ethics plan. Unique JHU ecosystem: APL + Medicine + IEEE/ICICLE partnership.
notes: Key anchor program. Direct connection to IEEE ICICLE credentialing pathway. Add to seed set for T13, T10.

name: Human-Computer Interaction Institute — CMU
description: Home of Ken Koedinger and Cognitive Tutor / LearnLab. One of the premier academic homes for LE research globally. PhD and MS programs.
notes: Koedinger is co-founder of learning engineering as a named field. Core institutional anchor.

name: Learning Engineering Virtual Institute (LEVI)
description: LEVI brought together practitioners and researchers to define learning engineering practice. Published foundational resources on LE process and evidence standards.
notes: Key grey literature source. Gap: verify current status and updated URL.

name: Open Learning Initiative (OLI)
description: OLI is the flagship LE platform from CMU — courses built with LE principles, continuous improvement, embedded formative assessment, and learning analytics.
notes: Landmark applied LE platform. Seed for T06 and T03.

name: Ken Koedinger
description: Co-originator of learning engineering as a field. Creator of Cognitive Tutor. Pioneer of PSLC DataShop and LearnLab. Foundational theorist and practitioner.
notes: Must-include anchor person. Seed multiple topics.

name: Ryan Baker
description: Pioneer in educational data mining and learning analytics. Author of key EDM methods handbook. EDM journal editor.
notes: Key anchor for T04, T06.

name: Bror Saxberg
description: Early champion of learning engineering as a named field. Wrote influential 2016 Atlantic piece calling for learning engineers. Practitioner-bridge figure.
notes: His 2016 article is a canonical entry point to the field. Tier 1 grey lit document.

name: Jim Goodell
description: Co-founder of Learning Engineering book (IEEE Press). Co-editor of canonical LE textbook. Core IEEE ICICLE contributor.
notes: Book co-editor. IEEE Press LE textbook is the primary seed document for the field.

name: John Behrens
description: Evidence-centered design; learning analytics at scale; psychometrics meets LE. Key bridge between assessment science and learning engineering.
notes: Key practitioner for T04, T09.

name: Learning @ Scale
description: Premier venue for LE research at scale. Emphasizes empirical evidence and adaptive/personalized learning. Annual.
notes: Core venue. Must include in expansion sources.

name: Educational Data Mining (EDM)
description: Annual international conference and journal. Primary venue for learning analytics and EDM research.
notes: Key venue for T04, T06.

name: Artificial Intelligence in Education (AIED)
description: Flagship AI in education conference. ITS sub-conference often co-located. Annual; alternating with ITS.
notes: Core venue for T06, T07.

name: Learning Analytics & Knowledge (LAK)
description: Premier analytics in education conference. Strong on data infrastructure and equity.
notes: Core venue for T04, T11.

name: IEEE ICICLE Annual Meeting
description: Annual community gathering for the IEEE learning engineering community. Standards, BoK, and credentialing discussions.
notes: Field-defining community event. T16 seed.

name: ASSISTments
description: Free ITS platform widely used for math tutoring research. Large research base. RCTs conducted on platform.
notes: Classic LE platform. Seed for T06.

name: DataShop / LearnSphere
description: Largest open repository of learning interaction data. Enables corpus expansion and field-level analysis.
notes: Critical infrastructure for LE research community.

name: xAPI / Tin Can API
description: Standard for learning activity data. Foundation for interoperable learning record stores. Key infrastructure standard.
notes: Standards anchor for T11.

name: IEEE ICICLE
description: International Community for IEEE Learning Engineering. Primary professional home for LE. Developing BoK, standards, credentialing. Resources page is a primary seed source for this corpus.
notes: PRIMARY external curation source. Resources page must be fully harvested.

name: Society for Learning Analytics Research (SoLAR)
description: International nonprofit dedicated to research, practice, and policy of learning analytics. Home of LAK conference and companion journal.
notes: Key community for T04.

name: International Society for the Learning Sciences (ISLS)
description: Home of CSCL and ICLS conferences. Bridges learning science and design. Important for T01 foundation layer.
notes: Key community for T01.

name: Learning Engineering (IEEE Press textbook)
description: The canonical textbook of the field. Two-part structure. End notes are a primary seed source for citation expansion. IEEE Press, 2022.
notes: PRIMARY seed document. Harvest all chapter end notes for corpus expansion.

name: IEEE ICICLE Resources Page
description: Curated resource list maintained by IEEE ICICLE community. Includes papers, tools, programs, and community resources. Lacks cohesion and learning journey structure — this corpus addresses that gap.
notes: Full harvest required. Categorize each item by content_type and topic_code.

name: How People Learn I & II
description: Foundational synthesis of learning science for educators. Free online. Widely cited. Essential T01 anchor.
notes: Free, authoritative. T1 seed.

name: Saxberg 2016 Atlantic: 'Forgotten Role of Learning Engineer'
description: Canonical entry point essay calling for the learning engineering profession. Widely cited. Short. Often assigned reading.
notes: Tier 1 seed even as grey lit. Critical for T00.

name: ICICLE Body of Knowledge (BoK) — current draft
description: The field's emerging canonical definition document. Defines competency areas, vocabulary, and practice standards.
notes: BoK version tracking required. Update when new version released.

title: The Job of a College President
description: The founding document of learning engineering. Simon coined the term 'learning engineer' and made the case that teaching effectiveness is a distinct, learnable expertise grounded in cognitive science — not a byproduct of subject-matter expertise. The philosophical foundation for everything that followed.

title: Online Education: A Catalyst for Higher Education Reforms
publisher: MIT Online Education Policy Initiative (MIT OEPI)
description: Re-injected 'learning engineer' into higher education discourse at the moment MOOCs reached 58M+ global students. Recommended universities create dedicated learning engineering roles. Catalyzed institutional adoption of LE language and practices across US higher education. Widely cited as the document that triggered the modern LE movement.

title: High-Leverage Opportunities for Learning Engineering
publisher: University of Pennsylvania Center for Learning Analytics
description: Synthesized a 2020 convening of 100+ academics, policymakers, and practitioners to define ten strategic priorities for the field in three domains: Better LE Infrastructure (shared R&D architectures, A/B testing at scale, reusable algorithmic components), Supporting Human Processes (teacher dashboards, predictive advising), and Better Learning Technologies (algorithmic equity, complex-skills measurement). The field's most comprehensive contemporary roadmap.

title: Artificial Intelligence and the Future of Teaching and Learning: Insights and Recommendations
authors: U.S. Department of Education, Office of Educational Technology
description: First major federal policy document addressing AI in education with explicit civil-rights framing. Documented algorithmic bias in e-proctoring systems (facial recognition failures for darker-skinned, transgender, and neurodivergent students). Mandated algorithmic fairness and data representation requirements that now set the ethical floor for learning engineering practice.

title: EDUCAUSE Horizon Report: Teaching and Learning Edition
description: The annual predictive literature for edtech adoption across global higher education. Tracks short-, mid-, and long-term adoption horizons. Documents the gradual mainstreaming of LE concepts (analytics, adaptive systems, AI tutors) from forecast to widespread adoption across the 2017–2024 arc.

title: NMC Horizon Report: 2017 Higher Education Edition
description: Identified adaptive learning technologies and data-driven personalization as short-term adoption trends in higher education. Forecast the long-term restructuring of degrees around competency measurement. Part of the annual Horizon series that documented the field's gradual integration into mainstream institutional practice.

title: Learning Engineering Toolkit: Evidence-Based Practices from the Learning Sciences, Instructional Design, and Beyond
description: The field's primary practitioner handbook. Frames LE as a verb — an iterative, evidence-based problem-solving process — not a technology set or a job title. Covers lean-agile methods, HCI design, data instrumentation, motivation modeling, and predictive analytics. Explicitly technology-agnostic: the principles apply equally to AI-driven software and low-tech interventions.

title: Learning Engineering for Online Education: Theoretical Contexts and Design-Based Examples
description: First book-length treatment of learning engineering applied to digital and distributed contexts. Saxberg's formulation — LE as applying learning science at 'massive, affordable, data-rich scale' — became widely adopted. Documents how online architectures enable continuous telemetry that peer-reviewed journals document years later.

title: Learning Engineering Adoption Maturity Model (LEAMM)
description: Organizational capability matrix allowing enterprises to measure fidelity of their LE adoption across human-centered design, learning science integration, and data-driven iteration dimensions. Provides a structured pathway from ad-hoc instructional design to mature learning engineering practice. The field's primary organizational self-assessment tool.

title: OpenSimon Toolkit
authors: Carnegie Mellon University, The Simon Initiative
description: A hybrid of grey literature, open-source software repository, and methodological guide comprising: DataShop (educational data repository), LearnSphere (world's largest open learning analytics infrastructure), CTAT (cognitive tutor authoring tools), OLI (Open Learning Initiative courseware), DiscourseDB, LightSide, Bazaar, Mathtutor, ChemCollective, and Echo. The flagship open infrastructure for learning engineering research globally.

title: 7 Things You Should Know About Learning Engineering
description: Widely circulated practitioner-oriented brief that introduced LE to a broad institutional technology audience. Part of the canonical EDUCAUSE '7 Things' series consumed by CIOs, provosts, and instructional technology directors. Accelerated LE vocabulary adoption in non-research higher education contexts.

title: IEEE ICICLE — Industry Consortium on Learning Engineering
description: Professional standards body for learning engineering. Produces the LE Adoption Maturity Model, competency frameworks, annual conference proceedings, and the Journal of Learning Engineering. Chartered 2017 under IEEE LTSC.
notes: The institutional anchor of the modern LE field. Sponsors the annual ICICLE conference; co-sponsors the xAPI profile work; primary source for LE body of knowledge documentation.

title: The Simon Initiative — Carnegie Mellon University
description: Cross-disciplinary learning engineering ecosystem at CMU. Encompasses LearnLab, OLI, DataShop/LearnSphere, and the METALS master's program. Mission: improve learning outcomes through rigorous research and technology deployment.
notes: Named for Herbert Simon. The world's most integrated LE research-to-practice ecosystem. Hundreds of faculty; world's largest educational log data bank; open-source tooling via OpenSimon.

title: Society for Learning Analytics Research
description: Academic society promoting learning analytics research, practice, and policy. Organizes the annual LAK conference and Learning Analytics Summer Institute (LASI). Publishes the Journal of Learning Analytics.
notes: Founded 2011 alongside the first LAK conference. Emphasizes practice-oriented, ethical analytics as distinct from pure EDM.

title: Penn Center for Learning Analytics
description: Research center at University of Pennsylvania directing EDM and LE policy work. Led by Ryan Baker. Produced 'High-Leverage Opportunities for Learning Engineering' (2021) and several landmark EDM papers.
notes: Key policy hub alongside academic research. Baker is editor of JEDM and a primary organizer of the EDM conference.

title: HCII — Human-Computer Interaction Institute, Carnegie Mellon
description: Academic home of learning sciences and technologies at CMU. Houses Ken Koedinger, Al Corbett, and the PACT Center. Offers PhD and MS programs integrating HCI, learning sciences, and data science.
notes: One of the premier academic homes for LE research globally. Co-originator of learning engineering as a named field.

title: International Educational Data Mining Society
description: Society organizing the annual EDM conference and publishing the Journal of Educational Data Mining. Focus on computational, discovery-oriented analysis of educational data.
notes: EDM emphasizes mathematical and algorithmic approaches; complementary to but distinct from LA (practice-oriented).

title: Learning Sciences Research Institute, UIC
description: Interdisciplinary research institute at University of Illinois Chicago spanning learning sciences, computational methods, and educational equity.

title: International Conference on Intelligent Tutoring Systems
description: Premier venue for ITS research: cognitive models, student modeling, adaptive instruction, feedback algorithms. Founded 1988 in Montreal. Proceedings published by Springer LNCS.
notes: The oldest dedicated LE/ITS conference. ITS2026 in Cyprus (June 1–5, 2026).

title: International Conference on Learning Analytics and Knowledge
description: Practice-oriented learning analytics, ethical deployment, dashboard design, predictive modeling, algorithmic fairness. Community-building for the SoLAR network.
notes: Emphasizes socio-technical context of analytics; complements EDM's algorithmic focus.

title: International Conference on Educational Data Mining
description: Algorithmic and computational approaches to educational data: knowledge tracing, early warning systems, automated feature engineering, student behavior modeling.
notes: One of three major computational LE conference series (with LAK and AIED).

title: International Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Education
description: AI-driven educational systems: generative AI tutors, NLP for learning, deep knowledge tracing, conversational agents, human-AI interaction in education.
notes: Largest first-authorship from China (vs. US dominance at LAK/EDM) — highlights globally distributed LE research ecosystem.

title: International Society of the Learning Sciences Annual Meeting
description: Design-based research, socio-cultural learning theory, collaborative learning, epistemology of learning environments. Balances computational rigor with qualitative and humanistic perspectives.
notes: Provides the qualitative and socio-cultural counterbalance to EDM/LAK's algorithmic orientation. Important for ensuring LE systems remain human-centered.

title: Learning @ Scale
description: Technical and pedagogical research on large-scale digital learning: MOOCs, online education platforms, at-scale experimentation, A/B testing in learning contexts.
notes: ACM-sponsored; focuses on the intersection of scale and learning quality. Home of 'doer effect' and A/B experimentation papers.

title: Journal of the Learning Sciences
description: Multidisciplinary forum for theoretical models, epistemology, and design-based research in learning sciences. Publishes foundational work on socio-cognitive frameworks, knowledge-building, and interaction analysis.
notes: Home of Brown 1992 (design experiments, 2,800+ citations) and Scardamalia & Bereiter 1994 (knowledge-building, 1,800+ citations). The theoretical anchor journal for learning engineering.

title: Journal of Educational Data Mining
description: Open-access journal dedicated to algorithms and methodologies for analyzing educational data: knowledge tracing, automated feature engineering, predictive student modeling.
notes: Premier venue for computational LE methods. Baker & Yacef 2009 (founding codification of EDM) published here. Diamond open access.

title: IEEE Transactions on Learning Technologies
description: Technical design and architecture of learning systems: adaptive algorithms, NLP conversational agents, multimodal telemetry (eye tracking, EEG, GSR), large-scale platform engineering.
notes: Requires substantive technical innovation in CS alongside pedagogical validity. Key venue for AI-in-education engineering papers.

title: Journal of Learning Analytics
description: Socio-technical implications of educational data: dashboard design, algorithmic ethics, data privacy, human-in-the-loop architectures, equity in analytics deployment.
notes: Official publication of SoLAR. Emphasizes practice implications and ethical framing alongside technical analysis.

title: International Journal of Artificial Intelligence in Education
description: Premier venue for ITS, generative AI in education, deep cognitive modeling, evaluation of AI vs. human tutoring. Algorithmic accuracy evaluations and human factors studies.
notes: Springer. The home journal for rigorous AIED research between conference cycles.

title: Journal of Learning Engineering
description: Applied integration of learning design, analytics, and systems thinking. Provides the 'Learning Engineering Primer' for terminological alignment. Diamond Open Access; community-driven by ICICLE.
notes: The only journal specifically dedicated to learning engineering as a named field. Diamond OA means no publication fees for authors or readers.

title: International Journal of STEM Education
description: Discipline-based education research (DBER): problem-based learning, flipped classrooms, educational robotics, STEM learning outcomes at scale.
notes: Springer Open. Critical venue for LE researchers designing domain-specific systems in STEM contexts.

title: IEEE 1484.12.1-2002 & P2881: Learning Object Metadata
description: Essential standards for cataloging, discovery, and semantic structuring of digital learning resources across repositories. Enables automated discoverability and cross-platform content reuse.
notes: Sponsoring body: IEEE LTSC. P2881 is the modernized successor incorporating linked data and accessibility requirements.

title: INCOSE — International Council on Systems Engineering
description: Global professional society for systems engineering. Publishes Systems Engineering journal and INCOSE Handbook. Bridges SE methodology and human factors in complex system design.

title: MIL-HDBK-46855A: Human Engineering Program Process and Procedures
description: DoD handbook for integrating human engineering into systems acquisition. Free, public domain. Directly feeds ICICLE Gov/Mil MIG work on mission-critical training design.

title: 1EdTech Comprehensive Learner Record (CLR) Standard
description: Open standard for capturing and sharing rich learner achievement data across systems. Successor to Open Badges. Underpins LE infrastructure for competency-based education.

title: Credential Engine / CTDL (Credential Transparency Description Language)
description: Non-profit mapping the credential landscape via machine-readable descriptions. CTDL schema enables interoperability between competency frameworks. Registry contains 1M+ credentials.

title: CASE (Competency and Academic Standards Exchange) Specification
description: 1EdTech specification for publishing, discovering, and correlating competency frameworks and academic standards. Enables machine-readable alignment between curricula and assessments.

title: Open Badges Specification (1EdTech)
description: Open standard for portable, verifiable digital credentials. Enables LE systems to issue machine-readable evidence of competency achievement.

title: Applied Research Laboratories (ARL) — University of Texas at Austin
description: U.S. DoD University Affiliated Research Center. Kevin Owens (featured in ICICLE Gov/Mil MIG webinar) conducts CTA-based training system design for mission-critical environments.

title: Journal of Cognitive Engineering and Decision Making
description: SAGE journal publishing CTA methodology, naturalistic decision making, and expertise research. Key venue for the Klein/Hoffman community.

title: Human Factors: The Journal of the Human Factors and Ergonomics Society
description: Premier venue for human factors and ergonomics research. Publishes CTA methodology, workload assessment, and human-system integration studies relevant to LE in complex domains.

title: ADDIE Model — Branson et al. (1975) Interservice Procedures for Instructional Systems Development
description: Original US Army ISD framework (Florida State University, 1975). Codified Analysis-Design-Development-Implementation-Evaluation as the canonical ID process. LE extends ADDIE with data-driven iteration.
authors: Branson RK, Rayner GT, Cox JL, Furman JP, King FJ
